text.p: I am delighted to be part of your success, no matter how small. Please feel free to use our correspondences or any other info you may need from me. You have my permission.
Pse: Biko echefukwala izitere m information gbasara journals ebe m ga- apublish papers m. Sendie ya taata

Nna a, kedu maka how far? you wouldn't kill me for not writing all this while, wud you? Believe me, no be my fault, though to some extent it is. Kedu maka ndi obodo unu that side? As for here, economic melt down is melting a lot of people. I hope Jehovah God is keeping you safe from the kind of fuel and other scarcities that have become Nigeria's other name. You dont want to be here at this point in time ,trust me.There is politics now everywhere, including on campus. Ndi obodo a si na ha ga-aburiri VC,or else...And the chairman of council has been bought over with twenty million! So,if you are Igbo and you love your life, I were nwayo tinye mma gi n'obo,gba nkiti. The university atmosphere is as calm as the grave yard. The watchword is trust nobody, talk to nobody. When are you coming home?

this is the second time i am writing this letter today.after the first one they started looking for their server before i could send it and I lost that one. How you dey and have you started your work? have you heard from the bank yet? If not I think what you suggested might be the ultimate solution. you send the letter to me and i will deliver it to the bank and follow it up to make sure they dispatch it on time .As for ,I finally got Bro < > on fone yesterday and he said his fone was stolen. the kids are ok and will vacate on the 5th of april. the university journal is finally out. your copy with the offprints are with me. I think by the grace of God I am ready for SL come october.That is if <> and < > do not come up with another trick.But God will lay an ambush for them this time round.
I think its better I end it here before they start looking for their server again.We shallhu.

I was on location throughout last week and could not respond to your mail until now, after my 5-6 lecture. Kedukwanu maka how far? Let me start with the Igbo program. You know the problems the Edo program has been having.You admit an Esan person because his name sounds Edo only for the person to ask for a transfer later. moreover,you know when it comes to the nitty gritty one needs to have done Igbo at the secondary level to be able to cope.It is not, in my opinion, advisable to just use proficiency for admission .That would create problems for us in the future. Have you forgotten the kinds of graduates the Edo program has been turning out? Moreover if Edo requires only proficiency in the language, it is understandable because Edo is not offered at the secondary level so the issue of credit or pass at that level cannot arise. What is more, can you imagine teaching Igbo to those born at Lagos or Kano who have never truly spoken Igbo but who are admitted into the program just because their name sounds Igbo? Or what do you tell a < > > from Benue, or < >< > from Cross River who may be admitted into Igbo because their names sound Igbo? I have never bought into the proficiency thing from the word go. But if it makes you feel better, I did not influence the admission in any way. I think Senate insisted on credit for admission into the program (I am not sure of this). All I have said are my personal opinion.

I am sure that you heard that < >'s boy < > died and resurrected (not after three days, anyway). Today it's < > that has been flown abroad for surgery. Who says Nigeria is not an exciting place to live? Have you got the mail from <> of UBA? I must confess I have not asked him of late since I came back from location and right now, My phones are FILLED with credit.
Please let me end it here and go and eat. I am now the only person left in the Department or even the faculty building at 6.55 pm. Ka chi fo.

Oga m,kedu maka how far? The people at are fine. Called them this morning and they are doing great.As for ,I lost a first cousin yesterday at a rather young age .She should be in her very early 50s and was the last daughter of my father's elder sister .Collapsed and died on her way to the market.Before that I was billed to travel home next weekend for the burial of my father's last survival sister who packed up in December at almost a hundred years .My cousin's burial has not been fixed.

The Igbo program is doing alright .They are about 9 so far.there are 2 courses for this semester,one of which they are taking in Social Sciences. I teach the other one, altogether teaching 5 courses, supervising 17 long essays,advising 300 level academically plus including complying their results and calculatingtheir GPAs. Add to that the fact that I am now the Departmental Exam officer as well as the Faculty Rep in the CED course offered in Social Sciences, and you have a perfect definition of a man who leaves home in the morning and does not get home until well after 7 in the evening. And do you know who the co-ordinator of the CED is? < >< >! Do I still have to tell you that I need your prayers? None of the candidates is from Delta because credit in Igbo was a factor in the admission. No new lecturers.Your oga has not made his feeling known to me but I know he will be particularly distressed because NO CANDIDATE was admitted for Edo.

Professor <>, ex-dean of Social Sciences was accused of plegiarism and invited to appear before a panel .Rather than appear he wrote a booklet accusing the VC of tribal persecution. He has since been suspended and he appealed to the one who eats okpa ewu. That one invited the VC and arranged < >< >'s thugs (No Exaggeration) to waylay the VC. Fortunately he did not travel in his official vehicle but was riding with someone else.They didn't know when he passed but later trailed him to the palace where theota okpa ewu accused the VC of marginalising Benin people. He ordered the Vc to reinstate <> and < > with immediately effected. They are yet to be reinstated.I have it on good authority that the baboon in Abuja has got the news and directed that <> be dismissed .The VC however insists that the law must take its course. < > has gone to court and the ota okpa ewu has requested the Chancellor,the olowo of to intervene. Uneasy calm rules the university now and every Benin student now believes that the Igbo are dominating them. Everything is at a standstill ,including convocation .Pray that you meet the university in one piece when you come back.Will call < > later.
Never, put your banana in the refrigerator!!!
After reading this, you'll never look at a banana in the same way again.
